texts.label: text
texts.orig: Journal of Health Informatics Research · Vol. 12, No. 3, 2024 · DOI: 10.1007/jhir.2024.0312
texts.text: Journal of Health Informatics Research · Vol. 12, No. 3, 2024 · DOI: 10.1007/jhir.2024.0312

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Twenty semi-structured interviews were conducted with adult patients (aged 28-74) attending a primary care clinic in a mid-sized urban centre. Interviews were audio-recorded, transcribed verbatim, and analysed using reflexive thematic analysis (Braun & Clarke, 2006). Four overarching themes were identified, each containing two to four sub-themes. Representative quotations for each sub-theme are presented in Table 1 below. Participant identifiers (P-01 to P-20) preserve anonymity.
texts.text: Twenty semi-structured interviews were conducted with adult patients (aged 28-74) attending a primary care clinic in a mid-sized urban centre. Interviews were audio-recorded, transcribed verbatim, and analysed using reflexive thematic analysis (Braun & Clarke, 2006). Four overarching themes were identified, each containing two to four sub-themes. Representative quotations for each sub-theme are presented in Table 1 below. Participant identifiers (P-01 to P-20) preserve anonymity.

texts.orig: Category rows (shaded) mark theme boundaries. Each data row corresponds to one participant quotation coded to a sub-theme.
texts.text: Category rows (shaded) mark theme boundaries. Each data row corresponds to one participant quotation coded to a sub-theme.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Expected behaviour: Table 1 preserved with 4 category rows and 12 data rows intact. Category rows (e.g. 'Theme 1: Information Access Barriers') appear as standalone rows that visually separate thematic groups. Final DataFrame: 16 rows × 3 cols (header + 4 categories + 12 data rows).
texts.text: Expected behaviour: Table 1 preserved with 4 category rows and 12 data rows intact. Category rows (e.g. 'Theme 1: Information Access Barriers') appear as standalone rows that visually separate thematic groups. Final DataFrame: 16 rows × 3 cols (header + 4 categories + 12 data rows).

texts.label: text
texts.orig: What the pipeline actually produces: stitch_split_cells() folds every category row into the Verbatim Quote cell of the data row that precedes it. Category rows have exactly 1 non-empty cell (col 0 = theme label, cols 1-2 = empty) - the same pattern as a genuine split-cell continuation. The function cannot distinguish them. Result: 'Theme 2: Trust and Credibility' gets appended to P-07's quote; 'Theme 3: Healthcare System Navigation' gets appended to P-14's quote; etc. The grouping structure of the table is silently destroyed. Final DataFrame: 12 rows × 3 cols (4 rows fewer, labels embedded in quotes).
texts.text: What the pipeline actually produces: stitch_split_cells() folds every category row into the Verbatim Quote cell of the data row that precedes it. Category rows have exactly 1 non-empty cell (col 0 = theme label, cols 1-2 = empty) - the same pattern as a genuine split-cell continuation. The function cannot distinguish them. Result: 'Theme 2: Trust and Credibility' gets appended to P-07's quote; 'Theme 3: Healthcare System Navigation' gets appended to P-14's quote; etc. The grouping structure of the table is silently destroyed. Final DataFrame: 12 rows × 3 cols (4 rows fewer, labels embedded in quotes).

texts.label: text
texts.orig: merger.py stitch_split_cells() lines 369-427. The loop at line 394 checks only: len(nonempty_idxs) == 1. There is no check for cell position (first column vs last), row height, content length, or whether the non-empty value is long enough to be a label rather than overflow text. Fix candidate: skip folding if the non-empty cell is in column 0 AND the cell text is longer than a configurable threshold (e.g. 20 chars), OR if the cell text does not share any tokens with the previous row's cells.
texts.text: merger.py stitch_split_cells() lines 369-427. The loop at line 394 checks only: len(nonempty_idxs) == 1. There is no check for cell position (first column vs last), row height, content length, or whether the non-empty value is long enough to be a label rather than overflow text. Fix candidate: skip folding if the non-empty cell is in column 0 AND the cell text is longer than a configurable threshold (e.g. 20 chars), OR if the cell text does not share any tokens with the previous row's cells.

table_cells.text: "I tried to look up my test results online but the patient portal kept logging me out. I gave up after the third time."
"My English is not perfect so I use Google Translate, but the medical words come out wrong. I am confused about what my doctor wrote."
"I live in a rural area. The internet connection is very slow. Downloading the health app took two days."
"I found five different answers on five different websites. How am I supposed to know which one is correct?"
"I only trust what my GP tells me. The internet is full of nonsense from people who are not doctors."
"After reading the side-effects list I stopped taking my medication. I wish someone had explained what was really likely to happen."
"I did not know I could ask for a second opinion. Nobody told me that was an option. I just assumed the first doctor was right."
"The referral process is completely opaque. I waited four months and nobody told me what was happening or why."
"Every department seems to have a different form. I filled in my details six times for the same admission."
"I felt embarrassed to admit I did not understand the diagnosis. I just nodded and left. I cried in the car."

table_cells.text: "My daughter helps me with all the online stuff. Without her I would be completely lost."
"When I got the diagnosis I went down a rabbit hole on YouTube for two weeks. It made my anxiety much worse."
